outcomes.description: Nature identical oils sustainably produced through precision fermentation.
Sustainable oils that are higher quality, higher consistency and price competitive to drop into existing supply chains for FMCG, Cosmetics, Nutraceuticals and Pharmaceutical companies.
Oils are created to specification and are a direct substitute for unsustainable oils to help companies secure supply, reach sustainability targets and meet regulatory requirements.
Capability, reputation and scale which delivers sustainable substitutions and target revenues and margins to Levur, its customers and investors AND creates significant climate impact.

delivery_pathways.content:
Develop proprietary processes and knowledge bank.

Develop proprietary platform for strain identification and development. 

Create continuous patenting process.

Recruit new roles: to run projects: Strain Dev, Synbio, Fermentation, DSP.

(grant/equity $)R&D Tax Credit: Ensure Team completes timesheets. 

R&D Commercially Focused: Mgt. communicate commercial outcomes for grants, investment, customers/projects.

Matrix Team: Ascertain what needs to be in-house v. outsourced.

Key Suppliers: Technical Team to develop relationships with key suppliers and feedstock providers.

AEA Project: Tech Team / CEO to establish waste-feedstock strategic partnerships through AEA Project.

Recruit Synthetic Biologists: to fast-track progress and manage new projects (eg. IGP OPO and BioSF MCT Commercialisation)

Continuous Learning: Online, Industry Seminars, specific courses

Training: Ensure team is utilising equipment to its full potential.

Proprietary Processes: Create proprietary processes for development and screening of strains.

Regulatory Pathways: Understand regulatory requirements for key categories. 

Engage external Regulatory Expert. 

Monitor regulatory changes in key markets Understand consumer sustainability expectations.

Leverage knowledge & resources available: what can we learn from others who've done it? 

Utilise relationships and connections. 

Ensure Team attendance and participation at industry events.

Database: Create a process and protocols for collecting and documenting data. 

Platform for strain identification and development Ipads & software in Lab: to document in real time.
CEO as CTO to drive the business and educate team and advisors.
Capture potential IP: Tech Team to document molecules that have commercial potential and could be patented.

Develop strategic roadmap: outlining priorities and timings; and investment required. 

Capital Strategy:  Seed and Beyond Grant Funding: Apply for relevant Grants for specific projects. 

Project Services: for short term revenue. Licencing Agreements: Capital light approach to create revenue stream. 

Create a saleable cosmetic ingredient with Yeast: Revenue

CRM: Create customer pipeline across categories and strategy 

Understand Customer Problems to Solve: and create Synbio solutions. 

Industry events (eg. ASCC): to network and create partnerships

Create samples: of end-products (tangible)Reverse engineer molecule solutions with end product benefits in mind. 

Clear briefs. Weekly R&D Meeting/QTR Updates: -Ensure Tech Team understands the market opportunities so they can identify potentially valuable solutions. -Ensure BD team understands technical capability.
Tours: Leverage Labs & Equipment in Business Development.
Understanding of regulatory pathways in each category as a business development angle: eg EUDR/ Scope 3
CEO & COO to utilise partnerships for industry insights and connections.

Ensure there is a mechanism for sharing technical findings with CEO/COO for potential commercial opportunities. 

Feedback Loop: R&D Meeting?

BD: Utilise advisors' connections and expertise in business. development. 

Formalise Advisory Committee. 

Governance requirements for Business.
Create bespoke Synbio ingredients that can be patented: multiple benefits etc. for customer differentiation.

Project Work: Customers to pay for project work and IP/rights to ingredient. Offtake Agreements: Secure Customers for offtake (if relevant).

JV: Levur do R&D and Customer Produce and Sell.

Early engagement with Customers to understand problems and opportunities. 

Co-creation of solutions with Customers Create panel of suppliers: CMOs, testing facilities, cosmetic chemists, ingredient suppliers, feedstock providers.

Project Team: Create internal project teams to oversee project end-to-end. Tech Team to speak to Client R&D team and present to Customers. Tech Team to keep on top of developments in industry/category.
Understand regulatory pathway prior to committing to projects. Utilise Customer regulatory resources where possible.
Ensure feedback loop between Tech Team and Commercial - to identify opportunities.
Utilise advisors in relevant technical fields: tech transfer, DSP, manufacturing.
Watermarked DNA Continuous Patenting (Protocol?)

Identify customer or market need & define target molecule profile. 

Relationship with DNA Supplier.

Scientific, management & operational process underpinning credibility & productivity Experiment methodology. 
Design DNA. 

Identify feedstock required. Integrate into proprietary yeast. Testing and optimisation with yield in mind.
Utilise equipment to fast-track development, screening, testing. High throughput.
Ensure an understanding of key regulatory requirements that might affect Strain Dev and Engineering (eg GMO) - and have impacts downstream.
Tech Team to utilise relationships with industry partners to fasttrack development.

A catalogue of solutions: catalogue all molecule profiles identified and learnings. 

Data and optimisation:  build faster & measure what matters. Continuous innovation platform.

Data-driven Decision Making: Focused, efficient data collection and information to support technical and business operations, enabling confident decision making 

Ensure regulatory and compliance requirements are communicated to Tech Team. 

Ensure expertise is brought in as required (eg. regulatory)

Assess scalability/ commercial viability with lab reactor: purity/value/yield - is it worth further investment? 

Invest in small batch reactor/tank to fasttrack development and testing

Budget for external scaling testing.

Fermentation Partner: Investigate partnerships with fermentation specialists at this small scale.

Feedstock Partnerships: Agri waste, agricultural, waste-feedstock.

Assess scalability/ commercial viability with lab reactor: purity/value/yield: Is it worth further investment? 

Identify right feedstock & refine process through testing and optimisation.

Consider purification methods.
Utilise high throughput equipment to assess strain efficiency and viability quickly.
Understand overarching regulatory requirements or constraints before commencing strain development.
Ensure all processes are documented and learnings shared.

Potential to create IP in waste-feedstock utilisation for Synbio lipids.

Potential to create proprietary feedstock to oil methodology.

Utilise AEA Grant to develop waste-feedstock project.

Waste-feedstock could significantly reduce costs of Synbio lipid production, dependent on supply chain.
Investigate potential feedstock suppliers/sources that will be commercially viable. Potential to partner with agri-waste producers to create circularity.
Identify and source feedstock for molecule yield, consistency, quality and commercial viability.
Utilise facilities and equipment to test feedstock prior to scaling/pilots.
Understand compliance and regulatory associated with feedstocks -including GMO and Scope 3. How does it affect Levur's positioning.
Synthetic Biologists to leverage learnings from industry / partners for efficiency.
Potential for proprietary fermentation and extraction methodology.

Pilot runs will be outsourced (Cauldron, Bio SG) and expensive: require capital.

Either BioSF funding or investment required to scale fermentation.

JV Opportunities: identify potential partners
Establish relationships with key fermentation companies: Cauldon and SG Bio- plus SE QLD Precision Fermentation facility.
Synthetic Biologists to upskill in understanding of scaling tech: fermentation and extraction.
Understand how lab testing (esp. 250ml machine) translates to pilot sized batches: accuracy.
Understand regulatory implications ahead of pilot runs.
Focused, efficient data collection and information to support technical and business operations, enabling confident decision making
Engage key advisors as required:- Regulatory- Tech Transfer- LCA/TCA
Opp. to create proprietary DSP methodology.
DSP likely will be outsourced to specialists and will be costly.

Potential to create partnership with DSP provider to create IP/Value.

Understand Customer needs/preferences - eg. Solvents like Hexane acceptability & impact to 'natural' positioning.
Ensure understanding of DSP from the beginning of strain design and throughout project - start with the end in mind.
Utilise existing equipment to understand DSP requirements.
Understand DSP regulatory implications.
Leverage industry partnerships/connections to understand DSP.
Engage specialists as required on DSP

Potential to create IP; eg. branded Levur ingredients or celebrity brand with Voice Capital.

Maintain core IP.
Consumer end-product: work with Customers on development, testing and production from end to end.
Team to understand application of technology/strain ahead of R&D.
Understand regulatory requirements for end-product category. Eg. testing requirements, GMO.
Continuous innovation platform. Data and optimisation. A catalogue of solutions.
Utilise cosmetic advisory committee to make connections and create commercial solutions.
Ensure anything that has potential as IP is documented.

Project Meetings with Customers: a feedback loop for improvement.

Ensure meetings are documented and learnings shared and filed for future use.
Promote a culture of continuous improvement and a growth mindset: testing, learning and optimising.

Understand capabilities of equipment to fully utilise for optimal outcomes. 

Advise Mgt of any capability gaps in equipment or knowledge.
Keep up to date on regulatory changes in Aust. in potential markets to ensure tech / products comply. Get ahead of regulatory changes as a competitive advantage.
Team to engage with relevant industry bodies and partners to learn and optimise their work.
Levur Proprietary Platform: ensure learnings, findings and molecules are documented immediately for reference.
Ensure customer experience with Levur is seamless by building network of reliable partners throughout value chain.

Ensure tech team engaged throughout project so technical challenges dealt with in real time.

Record keeping/ documentation key.
Work with Customers to ensure regulatory requirements/ documentation provided at each stage.
Leverage expertise of Advisory Committee for advice when required.
